text.title: Agency Tiered-Proposal Template System Core Rules
text.summary: 6 testable rules every application of `Agency Tiered-Proposal Template System` must enforce. Each rule pairs a binary check with a sourced rationale.

rule.statement: Every proposal MUST have exactly 3 tiers (Good / Better / Best). 2-tier (no anchor) and 4+ tier (decision paralysis) forbidden.

rationale.source: Ariely decoy effect (Predictably Irrational 2008); McKinsey B2B pricing research 2023

rule.rationale: Three-option pricing shifts the buyer from 'yes/no' to 'which one'. McKinsey data: 3-tier proposals close 35-45% vs single-price 18-25%. Two tiers loses the anchor; four triggers paralysis.

rule.statement: Middle 'Better' tier MUST be designed as the target choice. Good = 60-70% of Better; Best = 150-180% of Better. Scope: Better covers all stated needs.

rationale.source: Ariely decoy effect; HubSpot agency sales analysis 2024

rule.rationale: Middle tier is the strategy. Good prevents lowest-by-default; Best makes Better look reasonable. If 80% pick Good, raise Better's scope or lower its price; if 80% pick Best, Better is too thin.

rule.statement: Each tier MUST list exact deliverables + hours + SLA + out-of-scope examples. Vague phrasing ('ongoing support') without named hours/responses forbidden.

rationale.source: Mike Monteiro Design Is a Job; statement-of-work discipline

rule.rationale: Vague scope is the agency-founder's tax — every unclear sentence costs 2-5 hours of dispute later. Concrete scope = client signs what they want. Proposal IS the SOW.

rule.statement: Proposal MUST be sent within 48 hours of the discovery call. Past 7 days, buyer has often chosen a competitor.

rationale.source: HubSpot 2024 sales-velocity data; Win Without Pitching

rule.rationale: HubSpot data: proposals sent within 48h close at 2.3x rate of those at 4-7 days. 'I'm too busy' is the largest hidden cost in small-agency sales.

rule.statement: Every retainer tier MUST specify 6-month minimum commitment OR month-to-month with 20-30% premium. Shorter retainers under-deliver due to ramp-up.

rationale.source: agency retainer ROI analyses; Profit First retainer math

rule.rationale: Month-1 ramp eats value; compound effects emerge over months. 6-month minimum aligns vendor + client incentives. Month-to-month allowed only with explicit cost.

rule.statement: When preconditions in Applies If fail OR any item in Skip If triggers, do not run this methodology. Document the skip rationale in the artefact's parent log.
rule.rationale: Misapplied methodologies are worse than no methodology — they consume review cycles and produce false confidence.
